text:
International Football



Defending Olympic gold medalistsBrazilhave failed to qualify for the men’s football tournament at Paris 2024 following defeat toArgentina.

Argentina under-23s secured a 1-0 victory in Venezuela to qualify for this summer’s tournament at Brazil’s expense.

It means Brazil’s men’s football team will not be at theOlympicsfor the first time since 2004.

Advertisement

Argentinos Juniors forward Luciano Gondou scored the game’s decisive goal after 77 minutes, heading homeBrightonleft-backValentin Barco’s delivery.



South America’s two qualification spots for Paris 2024 were determined by theCONMEBOL pre-Olympic tournament in January and February.Paraguay qualified alongside Argentina after a 2-0 victory over Venezuela.

Atlanta forwardThiago Almada finished as the tournament’s top scorer with five goals.

The men’s football tournament at the Olympics consists of under-23 sides, with a maximum of three overage players allowed.

Brazil won consecutive golds at Rio 2016 and Tokyo 2020, beatingGermanyon penalties on home soil eight years ago, before defending their title with an extra-time win againstSpain. They had also medalled at the previous two games, taking silver at London 2012 and Bronze at Beijing 2008.

Argentina won consecutive Olympic golds at Athens 2004 and Beijing 2008, but have either failed to qualify or progress pass the group stages in each of the past three Games.

Argentina under-23 head coach Javier Mascherano was cautious over the chances ofLionel MessiandAngel Di Mariabeing included in the squad for the Paris Olympics given theirCopa Americacommitments but said there was “time to talk about it”.

Messi and Di Maria could both be selected as two of Argentina’s three over-23 players — although Di Maria ruled himself out of contention in January.

The pair were part of the Argentina side that won gold at Beijing 2008 alongside Mascherano but are already likely to compete in one major tournament this summer with the 2024 Copa America.

The Copa America takes place between June 20 and July 14, before the Olympics runs from July 24 to August 10.

“One understands the times for the senior national team: there’s the Copa America, it’s not easy,” Mascherano told TyC Sports. “For that reason we have to be cautious, obviously we’re going to talk about everything.

Advertisement

“You know my relationship with both Angel and Leo. We’re friends, we have a spectacular relationship. Me as a manager, obviously I have the obligation to invite them but one also understands that they have other commitments. It will also depend on that, it’s not so simple.

“Equally there is time to talk about it. Now the most important thing is to enjoy this, be grateful to this group of players, to these 22 who came here and who gave us the possibility not just for qualification but also having played these seven games in the way they played them.”

While the women’s football tournament at the Olympics consists of senior national teams, the men’s tournament is made up of under-23 sides. Athletes born on or after 1 January 2001 are eligible to play at Paris 2024.

Teams can also select up to three overage players in their 18-player squads.

Neymar was selected as an overage player for Brazil at Rio 2016 andSon Heung-minrepresented South Korea, while Spain midfielder Juan Mata went to London 2012 and Andrea Pirlo was in Italy’s squad at Athens 2004. The USMNT has consistently named a goalkeeper in one of the overage player slots, with Kasey Keller at Atlanta 96, Brad Friedel at Sydney 2000 and Brad Guzan at Beijing 2008.

The age limit for Tokyo 2020 was extended to 24 after the Games were postponed by a year due to the Covid-19 pandemic.

Qualification for the women’s Olympic football tournament is determined via senior international competitions; either tournaments in their own right, such as the Nations League or Copa America, or separate Olympic qualifying tournaments.

Given the tournament consists of under-23 sides, qualification for the men’s tournament is completed through various youth tournaments.

Advertisement

North and Central America qualification for Paris 2024 was determined through the 2022 CONCACAF under-20 Championship, the European spots came from the 2023 UEFA European Under-21 Championship, African nations qualified through the 2023 under-23 Africa Cup of Nations and Asian sides will be decided via the 2024 under-23 Asian Cup, which takes place in April and May.

Oceania and South America both host separate Olympic qualifying tournaments.

Fifteen teams progress to the games via qualification tournaments, while the hosts — in this case,France— qualify automatically.

(Federico Parra / AFP)

Ali Ramplingis a Deputy Managing Editor at The Athletic. Prior to joining The Athletic, she worked as the Women's Football Editor at 90min. Ali attended Loughborough University, and originates from Ipswich.Follow Ali on Twitter@AliRampling
title: Brazil fail to qualify for Paris 2024 Olympics following Argentina loss - The Athletic
author: Ali Rampling
